page: Microsoft Azure D11 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the D family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. It features 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with 1 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 14.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes 102 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results place the D11 in the lower tiers of performance. Its single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated as weak, while its multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10% cohort. Geekbench scores reflect similar trends, with a single-core score of 853.0 and a multi-core score of 1529.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show weak performance, with read-only small block cached speeds at 177.78 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 19.49 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks also rank in the lower percentiles, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 760.73 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 21.25 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D11 offers a moderate resource density with 7.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, but its older Intel Xeon hardware results in low benchmark-per-resource efficiency compared to modern instances. The presence of 102 GB of local storage provides utility for temporary files, but the overall weak processing and memory throughput limit its utility. This instance is suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small database operations, or simple background tasks that require more memory than standard entry-level instances but do not demand high computational throughput.
description: The Microsoft Azure D11 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance from the D family, equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. It provides 2 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 14.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates limited performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% cohorts. Memory bandwidth is also weak, with uncached large block writes at 19.49 GB/sec. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio but low computational efficiency, the D11 is best suited for lightweight database operations, basic static web serving, and low-intensity background processing where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D11 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 vCPUs, 14.0 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage. It delivers entry-level performance suitable for lightweight database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D11 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs, 14.0 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An entry-level virtual machine featuring a balanced memory-to-core ratio and local storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
14.0 GB of RAM providing a high 7.0 GB per core ratio
102 GB of local storage for temporary data and caching
Low-tier processing and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and background tasks
